title: Krova (Hutt)

'''Krova the Hutt''' was a minor [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime boss]] who operated from a base on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Alvorine]], in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Krova made herself an enemy of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] when she sold vital information to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Because of her, an entire contingent of Rebel soldiers were [[Massacre at Kolanda Station|slaughtered]] by the Imperials at [[Kolanda Station]]. Following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|fall]] of the Empire in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Alliance established a democratic [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and indicted Krova for war crimes, siccing [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] on her. Krova, however, arranged a fake capture and returned to Alvorine. Once there, she was attacked and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] by a band of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], who sought to get their hands on ancient [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] she retained.

==Biography==
Krova was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] at some point between 840 and 647 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Age" /> during the first [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] of the [[Great Peace of the Republic]]. Krova belonged to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="Gam5" /> a [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slug]]-like and long-lived race from the dying [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Varl/Legends|Varl]].<ref name="AGTSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> She embraced a career that some [[Human/Legends|Humans]] would qualify as criminal,<ref name="Gam5" /> although the morality of Hutts had little to do with that of many other species.<ref name="Star Wars: The Clone Wars">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' novelization]]</ref> Krova established her base of operations on [[Alvorine]], a [[Color/Legends|colorful]], [[Tree/Legends|forested]] planet<ref name="Gam5" /> located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="AOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref>

When a [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] broke out between the dominant [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] that fought against oppression, Krova understood that she could make more profit by selling information to both sides. Through the Hutt's double game, a number of routine missions turned into slaughters for the Alliance. Most importantly, Krova was personally responsible for the slaughter of many [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]] at [[Kolanda Station]], on the planet [[Latharra]]<ref name="Gam5" /> of the [[Yminis sector/Legends|Yminis sector]].<ref name="AOC" /> Thanks to the intelligence supplied by Krova the Hutt, the Galactic Empire was able to crush the Rebel troops that were sent there, cutting them down to the last man. That event was later known as the "[[Massacre at Kolanda Station]]." As a result, the Alliance officials declared Krova a war criminal and swore they would put her on trial as soon as possible.<ref name="Gam5" />

[[File:Alvorine.png|thumb|left|250px|Krova the Hutt around 4 ABY]]
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Empire was defeated at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and the Alliance turned into a new democratic government, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. As announced earlier in the war, the former Alliance members placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Krova's head due to her past connection with the murders of a multitude of Rebel personnel. Hoping that she could escape the punitive actions of the New Republic, Krova the Hutt left Alvorine in search of a safer hideout. During her escapade, she was ultimately captured somewhere near the [[Pluthan asteroid belt]] by a [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Langro Dis]]. However, it was believed that Krova herself had arranged the capture and cut a bargain with Dis to ensure her freedom. In any event, she subsequently returned to Alvorine.<ref name="Gam5" />

At that time, it was known that Krova had in her possession a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]], an ancient artifact built by the followers of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] religion. Being aware of that device's value, Krova had swallowed it, refusing to divulge its location to safeguard her freedom. However, unknown to the Hutt, the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] residing in her stomach began to emit a distress signal, attracting a group of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] led by [[Noth]], a [[Near-Human]] who resided in the wilds of Alvorine. The [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark siders]] confronted Krova, who was forced to turn the artifact over to them. The Hutt was later captured and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]], while her precious holocron, though damaged during Noth's attempt to steal it, remained in the Near-Human's possession.<ref name="Gam5" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Krova_the_Hutt_SWG5.png|thumb|150px|Krova the Hutt]]
Krova's physical appearance featured a few unusual traits for a Hutt. While most others were green with either red or yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="DBHutt">{{DB|species|hutt|Hutt}}</ref> she was a pinkish Hutt with a pale yellow underbelly and blue eyes.<ref name="Gam5" />

While she considered herself shrewd, cunning, and a good judge of other beings' character, Krova's confidence in her own skills proved to be misplaced more than once. She was difficult to read or influence through the use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]],<ref name="Gam5" /> an ability common to all Hutts and that caused worries to many [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] since time immemorial.<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref> Similarly, like many among her own kind, Krova could speak, read and write [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] in addition to her native [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].<ref name="Gam5" /> However, the Hutts were famous for assuming their racial and cultural superiority, and would rarely use another language than their own mother tongue.<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>
